name: Podio
description: A collaborative work platform for managing projects, CRM, and workflows with a RESTful API to automate and integrate Podio data and actions.

auth_methods.note: Documentation states Podio uses OAuth 2 (authorization code flow), supports client credentials (machine-to-machine) and accepts Bearer tokens; Podio does not use API keys for general access. Mapped 'client_credentials' to 'oauth2' and 'bearer_token' to 'token' to match allowed enum values.

credential_access.note: The Applications documentation states that to use the Podio API you must create an application, and when you create an application Podio generates a client_id and client_secret; it also states you can create an application from the Developer Portal after logging in (self-serve access to credentials).

api_surface.note: The Podio API documentation states the Podio API is a RESTful API over HTTP with JSON responses, and lists multiple resource areas (e.g., organizations, spaces, apps, items, tasks, users, contacts), indicating a broad REST API surface.

buildability.note: Official developer docs confirm a RESTful API endpoint at https://api.podio.com and self-serve creation of an application in the Developer Portal to obtain client_id/client_secret, supporting implementability today.
